author: RamSet
releaseNotes: 0.11.10 - Keepalive now also re-reads the undecoded custom characteristics so their raw values stay live in customParams (groundwork for exploring/decoding them). 0.11.9 - Exposes more of the HomeKit surface: fanState (actual fan running state - inactive/idle/blowing), thermostatAlert (ecobee alerts/reminders text), and the six per-profile setpoints (Home/Away/Sleep heat & cool). All read-only. 0.11.8 - Doc fix only: corrected the in-driver note about disabling the thermostat's motion/occupancy. To disable, comment the capability lines out and SAVE (not Import — importing overwrites the edit), and note it must be redone after any re-import/HPM update. 0.11.7 - The thermostat's own motion and occupancy (presence) are now enabled by default and reported live. To disable them, comment out the two capability lines in the driver and re-import. 0.11.6 - Every write command now updates its attribute instantly (optimistic) — setpoints, comfort profile, humidifier, fan min-on-time (mode & fan already did) — so the dashboard never lags a command, even for values the ecobee doesn't push events for. Remote-sensor battery added to live updates. (Driver files relocated to drivers/ in the repo; import URLs updated — re-import once from the new path.) 0.11.5 - thermostatMode now updates immediately when set by an app/rule/dashboard (optimistic update, like fan mode got in 0.11.3) — fixes the mode showing stale (e.g. 'Off') after a helper/rule changed it. The 5-minute keepalive now also re-reads the full thermostat state, so any update missed during a brief session drop (mode, setpoints, operating state, fan) self-heals within ~5 min. 0.11.4 - comfortProfile and holdEndsAt now self-refresh. Those values come from read-only characteristics with no event push, so the 5-minute keepalive now reads them too (along with fan min-on-time) — they update on their own instead of only after a manual Refresh. 0.11.3 - Fan mode now updates live. The driver now subscribes to TargetFanState (iid75) events, so an On/Auto change made elsewhere (webCoRE, the thermostat itself) reflects immediately instead of staying stale until a manual Refresh; driver-issued fan commands also update the attribute right away. 0.11.2 - Bug fix: in Heat/Cool mode the reported cooling/heating setpoint now reflects the ACTUAL target (HAP TargetTemperature) instead of the Auto-mode threshold — previously the displayed cool/heat point could disagree with what the thermostat was really doing. Thresholds are still used in Auto. Matches the existing mode-aware writes. 0.11.1 - Bug fix: fan mode was reversed ('On' set the fan to Auto and 'Auto' forced it On). HAP TargetFanState is 0=Manual/On, 1=Auto; the driver had it inverted on both the command and the status read. (prev 0.11.0: Reliability hardening over local HAP. Keepalive watchdog detects and reconnects a stalled/zombie live session; connect now retries on 'connection refused' (the ecobee briefly drops its HAP listener after a failed pair attempt); mDNS port discovery retries before falling back to the last-known port (the port can change after a reboot/power-cycle). Adds comfortProfile + holdEndsAt attributes (Home/Away/Sleep + active-hold end time) and a debug 'diag' flow trace. )
tags: Thermostat, ecobee, HomeKit, HAP, Local, Sensor

drivers.name: Ecobee HAP Thermostat
drivers.description: Local controller for an ecobee thermostat over the HomeKit Accessory Protocol (HAP): mode, setpoints, temperature, humidity, operating state, and fan.

drivers.name: Ecobee HAP Remote Sensor
drivers.description: Child device for ecobee remote sensors (temperature, occupancy, motion, battery). Created automatically by the thermostat driver.
